annotations.label: clinical finding
annotations.definition: A representation that is either the output of a clinical history taking or a physical examination or an image finding, or some combination thereof.

annotations.label: clinical history
annotations.definition: A series of statements representing health-relevant qualities of a patient and of a patient's family.

annotations.label: clinical picture
annotations.definition: A representation of clinically significant bodily components, dispositions, and/or bodily processes of a human being that is inferred from relevant clinical findings.

annotations.label: image finding
annotations.definition: A representation of an image that supports an inference to an assertion about some quality of a patient.

annotations.label: laboratory finding
annotations.definition: A representation of a quality of a specimen that is the output of a laboratory test and that can support an inference to an assertion about some quality of the patient.

annotations.label: preclinical finding
annotations.definition: A representation of a quality of a patient that is (1) recorded by a clinician because the quality is hypothesized to be of clinical significance and (2) refers to qualities obtaining in the patient prior to their becoming detectable in a clinical history taking or physical examination.

annotations.label: symptom
annotations.definition: A process experienced by the patient, which can only be experienced by the patient, that is hypothesized to be clinically relevant.

annotations.label: manifestation of a disease
annotations.definition: A quality of a patient that is (a) a deviation from clinical normality that exists in virtue of the realization of a disease and (b) is observable.

annotations.label: phenotype
annotations.definition: A (combination of) quality(ies) of an organism determined by the interaction of its genetic make-up and environment that differentiates specific instances of a species from other instances of the same species.

annotations.label: clinical manifestation of a disease
annotations.definition: A manifestation of a disease that is detectable in a clinical history taking or physical examination.

annotations.label: preclinical manifestation of a disease
annotations.definition: A manifestation of a disease that exists prior to the time at which it would be detected in a clinical history taking or physical examination, if the patient were to present to a clinician. A realization of a disease that exists prior to its becoming detectable in a clinical history taking or physical examination.

annotations.label: clinical phenotype
annotations.definition: A clinically abnormal phenotype.

annotations.label: disease phenotype
annotations.definition: A clinically abnormal phenotype that is characteristic of a single disease.

annotations.label: vital sign
annotations.definition: A physical sign in which a non-zero value is standardly considered to be an indication that the organism is alive.

annotations.label: predisposition to disease of type X
annotations.definition: A disposition in an organism that constitutes an increased risk of the organism's subsequently developing the disease X.

annotations.label: disease
annotations.definition: A disposition (i) to undergo pathological processes that (ii) exists in an organism because of one or more disorders in that organism.

annotations.label: genetic predisposition to disease of type X
annotations.definition: A predisposition to disease of type X whose physical basis is a constitutional abnormality in an organism's genome. This abnormality is the physical basis for the increased risk of acquiring the disease X.

annotations.label: acquired genetic disease
annotations.definition: A disease whose physical basis is an acquired genetic disorder.

annotations.label: constitutional genetic disease
annotations.definition: A disease whose physical basis is a constitutional genetic disorder.

annotations.label: abnormal homeostasis
annotations.definition: Homeostasis that is clinically abnormal for an organism of a given type and age in a given environment.

annotations.label: normal homeostasis
annotations.definition: Homeostasis of a type that is not clinically abnormal.

annotations.label: configuration
annotations.definition: A quality which is an spatial arrangement or distribution of a(n) independent continuant(s) across a Three Dimensional Region.

annotations.label: pathological physical configuration
annotations.definition: A configuration which deviates in some way from a canonical configuration for a particular organism.

annotations.label: disorder
annotations.definition: A material entity which is clinically abnormal and part of an extended organism. Disorders are the physical basis of disease.

annotations.label: epigenetic disorder
annotations.definition: A disorder whose etiology involves (1) a modification to the patient's genomic DNA which leads to alterations in the normal expression pattern of the genome, but is (2) not a change in the nucleotide sequence.

annotations.label: genetic disorder
annotations.definition: A disorder whose etiology involves an abnormality in the nucleotide sequence of an organism's genome.

annotations.label: acquired genetic disorder
annotations.definition: A genetic disorder acquired by a single cell in an organism that leads to a population of cells within the organism bearing the disorder.

annotations.label: constitutional genetic disorder
annotations.definition: A genetic disorder inherited during conception that is part of all cells in the organism.

annotations.label: clinical history taking
annotations.definition: A health care process in which a clinician elicits a description of previous sign and symptoms of disease from a patient or from a third party who is reporting on behalf of the patient.

annotations.label: clinical laboratory test
annotations.definition: A measurement assay that has as input a patient-derived specimen and as output a data item that is about a quality of the specimen.

annotations.label: physical examination
annotations.definition: A sequence of acts of observing and measuring qualities of a patient performed by a clinician; measurements may occur with and without elicitation.

annotations.label: etiological process
annotations.definition: A process in an organism that leads to a subsequent disorder.

annotations.label: pathological bodily process
annotations.definition: A bodily process that is clinically abnormal.

annotations.label: disease course
annotations.definition: The totality of all processes through which a given disease instance is realized.

annotations.label: chronic disease course
annotations.definition: A disease course that (a) does not terminate in a return to normal homeostasis and (b) would, absent intervention, fall within abnormal homeostatic range.

annotations.label: progressive disease course
annotations.definition: A disease course that (a) does not terminate in a return to normal homeostasis and (b) would, absent intervention, involve an increasing deviation from homeostasis.

annotations.label: transient disease course
annotations.definition: A disease course that terminates in a return to normal homeostasis.

annotations.label: diagnosis
annotations.definition: The representation of a conclusion of a diagnostic process.

annotations.label: normal value
annotations.definition: A value for a quality reported in a lab report and asserted by the testing lab or the kit manufacturer to be normal based on a statistical treatment of values from a reference population.

annotations.label: pathological formation
annotations.definition: TODO: Define, relate to disorder, and place in the OGMS hierarchy.

annotations.label: pathological anatomical structure
annotations.definition: An anatomical structure (FMA) is pathological whenever (1) it has come into being as a result of changes in some pre-existing canonical anatomical structure, (2) through processes other than the expression of the normal complement of genes of an organism of the given type, and (3) is predisposed to have health-related consequences for the organism in question manifested by symptoms and signs.

annotations.label: portion of pathological body substance
annotations.definition: TODO: Define, relate to disorder, and place in the OGMS hierarchy.

annotations.label: pathological transformation
annotations.definition: A pathological bodily process in which a canonical anatomical structure becomes a pathological anatomical structure.

annotations.label: pathological derivation
annotations.definition: A pathological bodily process in which matter is reorganized in such a way as to give rise to new pathological formations which take the place of entities existing earlier.

annotations.label: pathological invasion
annotations.definition: TODO: Define.

annotations.label: physical examination finding
annotations.definition: TODO: Define.

annotations.label: organism population
annotations.definition: An aggregate of organisms of the same type.

annotations.label: pain
annotations.definition: A bodily process in an organism S involving two integrated levels: (a) activation of the nociceptive system and associated emotion generating brain components of S, and (b) a simultaneous aversive sensory and emotional experience on the part of S, where (b) is phenomenologically similar to the sort of aversive experience involved in pain with concordant tissue damage.

annotations.label: syndrome
annotations.definition: A pattern of signs and symptoms that typically co-occur.

annotations.label: extended organism
annotations.definition: An object aggregate consisting of an organism and all material entities located within the organism, overlapping the organism, or occupying sites formed in part by the organism.

annotations.label: patient symptom report
annotations.definition: A communication from a patient about something they perceive as being abnormal about their body or life.

annotations.label: congenital disorder
annotations.definition: A structurally anomalous part of an organism acquired during fetal development and present at birth (but not necessarily hereditary) which is hypothesized to be harmful for the organism.

annotations.label: treatment
annotations.definition: A planned process whose completion is hypothesized by a health care provider to eliminate, prevent, or alleviate a disorder, the signs and symptoms of a disorder, or a pathological process

annotations.label: convalescence
annotations.definition: A processual entity during which a patient participating in a disease course gradually returns to participating in a canonical life course.

annotations.label: life course
annotations.definition: A process which has as parts all the processes in which a given organism is participant.

annotations.label: prognosis
annotations.definition: A hypothesis about some future part of a disease course.

annotations.label: acute disease course
annotations.definition: A disease course with an acute onset

annotations.label: inflammatory process
annotations.definition: A bodily process caused by some disorder that results in recruitment of leukocytes into a localized tissue site, typically causing localized pain and swelling.

annotations.label: health care process
annotations.definition: A planned process with the objective to improve the health status of a patient that directly involves the treatment, diagnosis, or prevention of disease or injury of a patient

annotations.label: health care encounter
annotations.definition: A temporally-connected health care process that has as participants an organization or person realizing the health care provider role and a person realizing the patient role.  The health care provider role and patient are realized during the health care encounter

annotations.label: hospitalization
annotations.definition: TODO
annotations.comment: 09/16/2020\nIs this same as admission? \nIs this the same as inpatient encounter?

annotations.label: outpatient encounter
annotations.definition: TODO

annotations.label: inpatient encounter
annotations.definition: A health care encounter involving a patient who has been admitted to a health care facility and remains in a hospital facility for at least one night.

annotations.label: emergency department encounter
annotations.definition: A health care encounter in which care is provided for undifferentiated and unscheduled patients with illnesses or injuries requiring immediate medical attention.

annotations.label: injury
annotations.definition: A disorder that involves some structural damage that is immediately caused by a catastrophic external force.

annotations.label: disease prophylaxis
annotations.definition: A planned process whose completion is hypothesized by a health care provider to reduce the risk of developing a disorder or the signs and symptoms of a disorder.

annotations.label: diagnostic process
annotations.definition: A health care process that involves the interpretation of a clinical picture from a given patient (input) and the assertion to the effect that the patient has a disease, disorder, or syndrome of a certain type, or none of these (output).

annotations.label: relapse
annotations.definition: A disease stage which is preceded by a remission and characterized by the return of a manifestation of a disease.

annotations.label: remission
annotations.definition: A disease stage which is characterized by the lack of a manifestation of a disease

annotations.label: ancillary health care process
annotations.definition: A planned process that has the objective to support the objective of a health care process without directly involving the treatment, diagnosis, or prevention of disease or injury of a patient.

annotations.label: health care process assay
annotations.definition: A health care process with the objective to produce information about the material entity that is the evaluant, by physically examining it or its proxies.

annotations.label: health care process biomaterial transformation
annotations.definition: A health care process which results in physical changes in a specified input material

annotations.label: health care process data transformation
annotations.definition: A health care process that produces output from input data

annotations.label: prophylactic treatment
annotations.definition: A treatment whose completion is hypothesized by a health care provider to prevent the signs and symptoms of a disorder or pathological process.

annotations.label: therapeutic procedure
annotations.definition: A treatment whose completion is hypothesized by a health care provider to eliminate a disorder or to alleviate the signs and symptoms of a disorder or pathological process.

annotations.label: chemotherapy procedure
annotations.definition: A therapeutic procedure that uses synthetic or naturally-occurring chemicals

annotations.label: immunotherapy procedure
annotations.definition: A therapeutic procedure that uses immune system derived entities.

annotations.label: physical therapy procedure
annotations.definition: A therapeutic procedure that uses physical conditioning

annotations.label: radiation therapy procedure
annotations.definition: A therapeutic procedure that uses radiation.

annotations.label: asymptomatic
annotations.definition: A disease stage characterized by the lack of symptoms associated with a given disease

annotations.label: onset
annotations.definition: A disease stage that marks the beginning of a disease

annotations.label: acute onset
annotations.definition: A disease stage with a rapid unfolding of signs and symptoms

annotations.label: complete remission
annotations.definition: A remission which is characterized by a prolonged lack of all manifestations of a disease

annotations.label: partial remission
annotations.definition: A remission which is characterized by a prolonged lack of some manifestations of a disease

annotations.label: recovered from disease
annotations.definition: A quality in which complete clearance of the disorder is attained; however, physiological 'memory' may persist

annotations.label: clinical data item
annotations.definition: A data item that is about a patient and is the specified output of a health care process assay or diagnostic process

annotations.label: routine health care encounter
annotations.definition: A health care encounter that is regularly scheduled for the purpose of health monitoring.

annotations.label: follow-up health care encounter
annotations.definition: A health care encounter that is scheduled for the purpose of assessing a known set of specific issues

annotations.label: congenital infection
annotations.definition: A congenital disorder arising as a result of an infectious process

annotations.label: drug induced congenital malformation
annotations.definition: A congenital disorder arising as a result of a drug exposure causing an anatomical abnormality

annotations.label: congenital process
annotations.definition: A pathological bodily process that occurs in the fetus during fetal development

annotations.label: physical sign
annotations.definition: An abnormal material entity that is part of a patient and hypothesized to be clinically relevant.

annotations.label: allergic disease process
annotations.definition: The process in which an allergic disease unfolds.

annotations.label: cancer process
annotations.definition: The process in which cancer unfolds

annotations.label: autoimmune disease process
annotations.definition: The process in which an autoimmune disease unfolds.

annotations.label: cancer progression stage 0
annotations.definition: A cancer progression stage characterized by abnormal cellular growth contained within normal tissue boundaries (e.g. carcinoma in situ).

annotations.label: cancer progression stage I
annotations.definition: A cancer progression stage characterized by abnormal cellular growth that extends through normal local tissue boundaries as a contiguous mass.

annotations.label: cancer progression stage II
annotations.definition: A cancer progression stage characterized by abnormal cellular growth that extends well beyond normal local tissue boundaries as a contiguous mass.

annotations.label: cancer progression stage III
annotations.definition: A cancer progression stage characterized by abnormal cellular growth that extends through normal local tissue boundaries as a contiguous mass and includes metastasis to one or more regional draining lymph nodes.

annotations.label: cancer progression stage IV
annotations.definition: A cancer progression stage characterized by abnormal cellular growth that extends through normal local tissue boundaries as a contiguous mass and includes metastasis to other distant tissues besides lymph nodes.

annotations.label: infectious disease process
annotations.definition: The process in which an infectious disease unfolds

annotations.label: processual sign
annotations.definition: An abnormal processual entity occuring in a patient that is hypothesized to be clinically relevant.

annotations.label: qualitative sign
annotations.definition: An abnormal observable quality of a part of a patient that is hypothesized to be clinically relevant.

annotations.label: transplantation
annotations.definition: A therapeutic procedure in which a body part is transferred into a organism

annotations.label: solid organ transplant
annotations.definition: A transplantation in which the body part is a non-fluid tissue

annotations.label: transfusion
annotations.definition: A transplantation in which the body part is a bodily fluid

annotations.label: tissue disorder
annotations.definition: A disorder of some macroscopic part of a tissue

annotations.label: neoplasm
annotations.definition: A tissue disorder that has resulted from a dysregulation of cell proliferation leading to a net increase in mass

annotations.label: medication role
annotations.definition: A role borne by some material entity which can be delivered/administrated into some organism and the role is realized during a clinical treatment process aiming to treat symptoms, signs or diagnosed disease.
